organizations.notes: Longtime Austin civic figure and philanthropist who runs MDW Interests, a private oil, gas and real estate investment firm registered in Austin. Co-founder and president of the Waller Creek Conservancy (now Waterloo Greenway), former facilities chair at St. Edward's University, and instrumental in the merger creating The Contemporary Austin. Despite the 'Unemployed / None' occupation on the filing, her business identity is documented in public press releases. Her 213 federal contributions with a near-uniform Democratic lean are consistent with the civic-donor profile; no ideological PAC tie (pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun, or defense) was found in public records.

Alison Alter was the sitting Austin City Council Member for District 10 in November 2021, living in the Rosedale neighborhood of Austin; she founded the philanthropic-advising firm Alter Advising in 2013 and holds a Ph.D. from Harvard and a B.A. from Stanford. The name is uncommon in Austin, and a $125 gift from a sitting Democratic council member to Travis County Commissioner Jeff Travillion ahead of his 2022 primary, plus an FEC lean of 1.0 across 57 donations, is consistent with her profile. Confidence is medium rather than high because the donor record carries no ZIP, so identity rests on name rarity and donation pattern rather than an address match. Not a registered Texas lobbyist (the only similar TEC entry is 'Edwards, Alison T.').

President & CEO of the Texas Hotel & Lodging Association (THLA) for 24+ years, the largest state hotel association; leads legislative advocacy for the Texas lodging industry. Austin 78701, matches donor record.

organizations.org: Greater Austin Asian Chamber of Commerce
organizations.notes: Same person confirmed: Ali Khataw's own Encotech resume page lists 'Ali Khataw Photography (1975-Present)' as his photo-philanthropy practice alongside his role as founder/President-CEO of Encotech Engineering Consultants in Austin, resolving the filing's 'CEO / Ali Khataw Photography' occupation. Zip 78746 (West Lake Hills) is consistent with an Austin engineering-firm principal. FEC records list him as CEO, Encotech Engineering, giving to Democratic committees (e.g., $1,000 to Hillary Victory Fund, 9/28/2016), consistent with the 1.0 partisan lean. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

Barrett Lepore is an Associate at Austin-based developer Riverside Resources; named in trade/news coverage overseeing projects including The Whitley, Fifth & West, and the Clarksvillage development on West Sixth St. Resides 78746. Not in TX lobbyist registry; no PAC ties found.

organizations.org: Greater Austin Hispanic Chamber of Commerce / Greater Austin Chamber of Commerce
organizations.role: Former Board Chair (GAHCC); former Executive Board member (Greater Austin Chamber)
organizations.notes: Paul M. Saldana, president/principal of Saldana Public Relations in Austin; former Austin ISD Trustee for District 6 (2015-2017) and Board Vice-President (2016-17), and former chief of staff to Austin Mayor Gus Garcia. Occupation ('PR Consultant'), employer string ('Saldana PR') and south-Travis-County location (Manchaca 78652) all corroborate. Confirmed NOT a Texas Ethics Commission registered lobbyist (the only Saldana in TEC lobby records is Amanda Saldana of Edinburg) and no match in the City of Austin City Clerk registered-lobbyist list.

Jessica V. Huynh is an Austin criminal-defense trial attorney who runs JVH Law, PLLC (self-employed, matching the filed employer "Self" + occupation "Attorney"); she spent 11 years as a prosecutor in Galveston, Williamson and Travis counties, was lead trainer at the Travis County DA's Office, and is an adjunct at UT Law. Zip 78737 (southwest Austin on the Travis/Hays line) is corroborated by her listed membership in the Hays County Bar Association alongside Austin-area bar groups. She was the 2022 Democratic primary candidate for Travis County's 331st District Court, consistent with the pipeline's FEC match showing 9 federal contributions at a fully Democratic partisan lean. FEC and TEC searches surfaced no federal ideological-PAC giving and no Texas lobbyist registration.

Best match is Gary Allan Cobb, longtime Travis County assistant district attorney (24 years, director of the Grand Jury/Intake Division), UT Law 1986, who ran in the 2008 and 2016 Democratic primaries for Travis County DA and said after losing to Margaret Moore in 2016 that he would not stay at the office. Zip 78749 is corroborated by contemporaneous reporting describing him as vice president of the Southwest Austin Democrats (78749 is southwest Austin) and by a City of Austin filing listing a 512-899 (southwest Austin) home number. 'Retired' occupation and a heavily Democratic 92-donation federal giving history (FEC lean 0.90) are consistent with his documented Democratic-primary political activity. Confidence held at medium because no public record directly ties the 78749 address to this specific Gary Cobb, and there are other Austin-area Gary Cobbs (including one whose obituary appears in 2019). Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile and Martindale/State Bar listings found no lobbying, energy, firearms, defense, or Israel/Palestine-related roles on any side; no TEC lobbyist registration found.

Confirmed as former Austin City Council Member Laura Morrison: FEC contribution records list Laura Morrison at Austin ZIP 78756 with employer 'Texas Electric Transportation Resource' and occupation 'Director of Community Initiatives,' and TxETRA's own staff page identifies that Laura Morrison as the former Lockheed engineer who 'served two terms on the Austin City Council.' Her name no longer appears on the successor org's current team page, consistent with 'RETIRED' on the 2026 filing.

Richard 'Rick' Cofer, managing partner of Cofer & Connelly, PLLC (602 W 11th St, Austin 78701 — matches the donor zip and the employer on the filing). Former Travis County assistant county attorney and assistant district attorney; long-serving Austin city board/commission member and a well-known figure in Travis County Democratic politics (he was the front-running, later withdrawn, candidate for Travis County Democratic Party chair in 2017), consistent with the input's 0.99 Democratic partisan lean. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found for him in TEC registered-lobbyist lists.

Matches the well-documented Selena Xie, an ATCEMS paramedic who served six years as President of the Austin EMS Association, the union representing 500+ Austin paramedics, and who negotiated three City of Austin labor contracts. Occupation 'Paramedic' and employer 'ATCEMS' in the filing match her public record exactly; a Texas Ethics Commission campaign finance filing separately lists her as 'Xie, Selena — Paramedic, ATCEMS.' She is herself a 2026 candidate for Austin City Council District 8, so a fuller public record legitimately exists. Classified as Labor rather than Government because her policy-relevant public role is union leadership.

organizations.role: County Commissioner, 1995–2026 (retired); previously Travis County Constable from 1980
organizations.notes: Identity confirmed by donation-history continuity at a single address in City of Austin campaign finance data: 'Gomez, Margaret', Austin TX 78704 / 78704-5950, listed as occupation 'County Commissioner', employer 'Travis County' on contributions in 2024 (to Gary Bledsoe) and 2025-12-03 (to Marc Duchen), then as 'Retired' on the 2026-04-07 $450 contribution to Steven A. Brown — matching her 2026 retirement. This is Margaret Gómez, the longest-serving commissioner in Travis County history, first elected commissioner in 1995 and the first Mexican-American woman in the role; she stepped down in June 2026 after nearly 53 years as a county employee. Zip 78704 is consistent with Precinct 4, which covers nearly all of South Austin east of South Lamar. Not listed in Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists for 2021–2026. The project's FEC match shows lean 1.0 (Democratic) across 928 federal donations, consistent with a longtime Democratic officeholder's recurring small-dollar giving; specific committees could not be enumerated this session because the OpenFEC API returned HTTP 429 on every attempt, and no PAC ties in any tracked category surfaced via web search.

organizations.org: Carmen Llanes Pulido for Mayor of Austin (2024)
organizations.notes: Carmen Llanes Pulido, native East Austinite (donor zip 78722 is East Austin), has been Executive Director of Go Austin/Vamos Austin (GAVA) since March 2017 and ran for Mayor of Austin in 2024, finishing second with about 20% of the vote. Filing employer/occupation ('Go Austin Vamos Austin (GAVA)', 'Executive Director / Nonprofit Executive Director') matches exactly. She previously organized with PODER (People Organized in Defense of Earth and her Resources) in East Austin and ran a fitness/nutrition program at Marathon Kids. FEC search returned no federal PAC contributions under her name; her documented political finance activity is her local Austin mayoral committee (~$71,000 raised through July 2024), which is city-level, not federal. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration under 'Llanes' was found. Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile, InfluenceWatch and UT School of Architecture event bio found extensive local civic and land-use advocacy but no Israel/Palestine, gun, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations on either side of those spectrums.

Ora Houston is the first person to hold the Austin City Council District 1 seat (2015-2019), a lifelong resident of the Blackland neighborhood in East Austin (zip 78722). Occupation 'Retired' and the 78722 zip match her documented residence; her $400 gift to Steven Brown is in the District 1 seat she formerly held. No FEC ideological-PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration surfaced.

organizations.role: Founder, President & General Counsel; registered lobbyist
organizations.notes: Jack Gullahorn is a prominent Austin lobbyist and attorney who founded and led (15 yrs) the Professional Advocacy Association of Texas (PAAT), the lobbyists' trade association. Distinctive name; a well-known Capitol figure. Gave to Travillion.

Paul M. Saldana, president/principal of Saldana Public Relations in Austin; former Austin ISD Trustee for District 6 (2015-2017) and Board Vice-President (2016-17), and former chief of staff to Austin Mayor Gus Garcia. Occupation ('PR Consultant'), employer string ('Saldana PR') and south-Travis-County location (Manchaca 78652) all corroborate. Confirmed NOT a Texas Ethics Commission registered lobbyist (the only Saldana in TEC lobby records is Amanda Saldana of Edinburg) and no match in the City of Austin City Clerk registered-lobbyist list.

Reported occupation 'Director' and employer 'PODER' match exactly a well-documented East Austin public-record figure: Susana Almanza co-founded PODER in 1991 and serves as its director. She is a longtime environmental-justice organizer, a two-time Austin City Council District 3 candidate (2014, 2018), and a 2021 appointee to the National Environmental Justice Advisory Council. Her documented advocacy is environmental justice, anti-gentrification and land use in East Austin; no federal PAC, lobbyist-registry, or Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas, or defense-industry affiliations were found on either side of those spectrums.

Jessica V. Huynh is an Austin criminal-defense trial attorney who runs JVH Law, PLLC (self-employed, matching the filed employer "Self" + occupation "Attorney"); she spent 11 years as a prosecutor in Galveston, Williamson and Travis counties, was lead trainer at the Travis County DA's Office, and is an adjunct at UT Law. Zip 78737 (southwest Austin on the Travis/Hays line) is corroborated by her listed membership in the Hays County Bar Association alongside Austin-area bar groups. She was the 2022 Democratic primary candidate for Travis County's 331st District Court, consistent with the pipeline's FEC match showing 9 federal contributions at a fully Democratic partisan lean. FEC and TEC searches surfaced no federal ideological-PAC giving and no Texas lobbyist registration.

organizations.org: Texas Women's Political Caucus / Raza Unida Party (Mujeres de La Raza Unida)
organizations.notes: Martha P. Cotera (b. 1938), longtime South Austin resident and nationally documented Chicana feminist organizer: co-founder of the Texas Women's Political Caucus (1973), founder of the Chicana Research and Learning Center in Austin (1974), co-founder of Raza Unida Party and of the Greater Austin Hispanic Chamber of Commerce and the Emma S. Barrientos Mexican American Cultural Center. Identity corroborated by distinctive name, Austin residence in public records, all-Democratic federal giving pattern (69 recorded federal gifts, lean 1.0) consistent with her documented lifelong Democratic/Chicano-movement activism, and her service on a City of Austin commission. City of Austin proclaimed 'Martha P. Cotera Day' on Aug 25, 2023.

Alison Alter was the sitting Austin City Council Member for District 10 in November 2021, living in the Rosedale neighborhood of Austin; she founded the philanthropic-advising firm Alter Advising in 2013 and holds a Ph.D. from Harvard and a B.A. from Stanford. The name is uncommon in Austin, and a $125 gift from a sitting Democratic council member to Travis County Commissioner Jeff Travillion ahead of his 2022 primary, plus an FEC lean of 1.0 across 57 donations, is consistent with her profile. Confidence is medium rather than high because the donor record carries no ZIP, so identity rests on name rarity and donation pattern rather than an address match. Not a registered Texas lobbyist (the only similar TEC entry is 'Edwards, Alison T.').

organizations.role: Tax Assessor-Collector and county voter registrar (former)
organizations.notes: Travis County Archives' elections exhibit documents Cecelia Burke as Travis County Tax Assessor-Collector (pictured at a 1989 voter registration drive); she preceded Bruce Elfant, who took office in 2013. The donor's Austin 78731 residence and extremely heavy, near-uniformly Democratic federal giving pattern (533 contributions, partisan lean 0.999) are consistent with a retired countywide Democratic officeholder, but no source directly ties the 78731 donor record to the former officeholder, so identity is probable rather than confirmed. No TEC lobbyist registration; no PAC or advocacy-org tie on any side of the searched spectrums.

Best match is Gary Allan Cobb, longtime Travis County assistant district attorney (24 years, director of the Grand Jury/Intake Division), UT Law 1986, who ran in the 2008 and 2016 Democratic primaries for Travis County DA and said after losing to Margaret Moore in 2016 that he would not stay at the office. Zip 78749 is corroborated by contemporaneous reporting describing him as vice president of the Southwest Austin Democrats (78749 is southwest Austin) and by a City of Austin filing listing a 512-899 (southwest Austin) home number. 'Retired' occupation and a heavily Democratic 92-donation federal giving history (FEC lean 0.90) are consistent with his documented Democratic-primary political activity. Confidence held at medium because no public record directly ties the 78749 address to this specific Gary Cobb, and there are other Austin-area Gary Cobbs (including one whose obituary appears in 2019). Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile and Martindale/State Bar listings found no lobbying, energy, firearms, defense, or Israel/Palestine-related roles on any side; no TEC lobbyist registration found.

organizations.notes: Paul M. Saldana, president/principal of Saldana Public Relations in Austin; former Austin ISD Trustee for District 6 (2015-2017) and Board Vice-President (2016-17), and former chief of staff to Austin Mayor Gus Garcia. Occupation ('PR Consultant'), employer string ('Saldana PR') and south-Travis-County location (Manchaca 78652) all corroborate. Confirmed NOT a Texas Ethics Commission registered lobbyist (the only Saldana in TEC lobby records is Amanda Saldana of Edinburg) and no match in the City of Austin City Clerk registered-lobbyist list.

organizations.org: Texas Campaign for the Environment / TCE Fund
organizations.notes: Austin 78704 and the filing's 'Not Employed' match Robin Schneider, the longtime Executive Director of Texas Campaign for the Environment, who retired in April 2025 after roughly 25 years leading the group's anti-pollution and recycling advocacy (per her organizational bio and a LinkedIn listing her as retired as of April 2025). FEC records for 'Robin Schneider, Austin, TX' with employer 'Texas Campaign for the Environment' and occupation 'Executive Director' shift to 'Not Employed' beginning in late 2025, consistent with that retirement date. A same-named Robin Schneider in Castroville, TX is a different person and not this donor.

organizations.org: Central Health performance-audit advocacy (Travis County Commissioners Court)
organizations.role: Public advocate / speaker before Commissioners Court
organizations.notes: Almost certainly Susan A. Spataro, CPA, who served ~24 years as Travis County Auditor until Travis County district judges removed her in August 2012; she is publicly described in Austin news coverage as 'retired Travis County Auditor' and remains an active Austin civic voice (Central Health audit advocacy). Corroborated by name + Austin location + 'RETIRED' occupation on the filing; the news sources do not state her zip, so identity is strong but not zip-confirmed.

Longtime Austin civic figure and philanthropist who runs MDW Interests, a private oil, gas and real estate investment firm registered in Austin. Co-founder and president of the Waller Creek Conservancy (now Waterloo Greenway), former facilities chair at St. Edward's University, and instrumental in the merger creating The Contemporary Austin. Despite the 'Unemployed / None' occupation on the filing, her business identity is documented in public press releases. Her 213 federal contributions with a near-uniform Democratic lean are consistent with the civic-donor profile; no ideological PAC tie (pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun, or defense) was found in public records.

organizations.role: Instrumental in creation of Arthouse at the Jones Center / The Contemporary Austin
organizations.notes: Longtime Austin civic figure and philanthropist who runs MDW Interests, a private oil, gas and real estate investment firm registered in Austin. Matches the well-documented Selena Xie, an ATCEMS paramedic who served six years as President of the Austin EMS Association, the union representing 500+ Austin paramedics, and who negotiated three City of Austin labor contracts. Occupation 'Paramedic' and employer 'ATCEMS' in the filing match her public record exactly; a Texas Ethics Commission campaign finance filing separately lists her as 'Xie, Selena — Paramedic, ATCEMS.' She is herself a 2026 candidate for Austin City Council District 8, so a fuller public record legitimately exists. Classified as Labor rather than Government because her policy-relevant public role is union leadership.

organizations.org: East Riverside/Oltorf Combined Neighborhood Planning Area Contact Team
organizations.notes: Retired after ~40 years in Information Systems/Networking Engineering; designed transport technologies on the City of Austin metropolitan fiber network. Lives in 78741; chairs the East Riverside/Oltorf Neighborhood Plan Contact Team. Occupation 'Engineer'/'retired' and zip match. (retired)
